messages.content: Are the PG Diploma courses the same as the PG Certificate courses offered by C-DAC?

Yes, the PG Certificate Programmes are the same courses that were earlier known as PG Diploma programmes. As mentioned in the admission booklet, C-DAC has rebranded its Post Graduate Diploma Courses as Post Graduate Certificate Programmes, such as PG-DAC now being referred to as PGCP-AC. Is there anything I can help with?
Where does C-DAC conduct its training programmes?

C-DAC conducts its training programmes through centres located across multiple cities in India, including Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad, Pune, Mumbai, New Delhi, and others. Programmes are also offered through authorised training centres. Would you like details of centres offering a specific course?
I want details of centres offering a specific PG Certificate Programme.
Please specify the PG Certificate Programme name to list the centres where it is offered.
What kind of programmes are the PG Certificate Programmes offered by C-DAC?

The PG Certificate Programmes are full-time, postgraduate-level professional training programmes focused on advanced computing and emerging IT domains. They are designed to build practical and industry-relevant skills. Would you like to know which specific programmes are offered in the February 2026 batch?
Which PG Certificate Programmes are offered by C-DAC for the February 2026 batch?

For the February 2026 batch, C-DAC is offering multiple PG Certificate Programmes across areas such as Advanced Computing, Big Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security, Embedded Systems, VLSI Design, HPC System Administration, and several other advanced IT domains. Would you like the complete list of programme names?
Give me the complete list of PG Certificate Programme names.

The PG Certificate Programmes offered include Advanced Computing, Big Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, IT Infrastructure Systems & Security, Mobile Computing, Advanced Secure Software Development, HPC System Administration, FinTech & Blockchain Development, Cyber Security & Forensics, Robotics & Allied Technologies, Embedded Systems Design, and VLSI Design. Let me know if you need any help.
Are these degree courses or certificate programmes?

These are Post Graduate Certificate Programmes and not degree courses. They are professional training programmes delivered through C-DAC’s training centres. Would you like to know how long these programmes run?
How long do the PG Certificate Programmes run?
Each PG Certificate Programme is conducted over a total duration of 24 weeks. Is there anything I can help with?
Who are these PG Certificate Programmes suitable for?

These programmes are suitable for graduates and postgraduates from engineering, computer science, and related disciplines who want to build advanced technical skills for the ICT industry. Eligibility depends on the specific programme. Would you like help checking eligibility for a particular course?
Are the PG Certificate Programmes full-time or part-time?

The PG Certificate Programmes are full-time courses and require regular daily participation in classes, labs, and related activities. Students are expected to be fully available during the course duration. Would you like to know the total duration of these programmes?
What is the duration of the PG Certificate Programmes?

Each PG Certificate Programme is conducted over a duration of 24 weeks and includes theory sessions, lab work, projects, and self-study components. Would you like a breakdown of how this time is distributed?
Are these programmes designed to help with job opportunities?

Yes, the PG Certificate Programmes are designed to develop industry-relevant skills, and C-DAC provides placement support through its Common Campus Placement Programmes. Is there anything you want to know about?
How many PG Certificate Programmes does C-DAC offer in this batch?
In the February 2026 batch, C-DAC is offering 12 different PG Certificate Programmes covering various specialisations in advanced computing and IT. Let me know if you need any help.
Are all PG Certificate Programmes available at every C-DAC training centre?

No, all PG Certificate Programmes are not available at every training centre. The availability of each programme depends on the centre, and seat capacity varies by location. These details are provided in the admission booklet. Would you like to check which centres offer a specific programme?
Can I apply for any PG Certificate Programme regardless of my educational background?

No, eligibility for PG Certificate Programmes depends on your educational background and the specific programme you apply for. Each programme has defined eligibility criteria mentioned in the admission booklet. Would you like to check eligibility for a particular programme?
I want to check eligibility for a specific PG Certificate Programme.
Please specify the PG Certificate Programme name so that the eligibility criteria can be stated.
Are any PG Certificate Programmes offered only in online mode?

Yes, according to the admission booklet, some PG Certificate Programmes are offered only in fully online mode, while others are delivered in fully physical mode or both, depending on the programme and centre. Would you like to know the mode of delivery for a specific programme?
Are there PG Certificate Programmes that are conducted only in physical mode?

Yes, several PG Certificate Programmes are conducted only in physical mode at training centres, as specified in the admission booklet. These programmes require on-campus participation. Would you like to know which programmes fall under this category?
How do I decide which PG Certificate Programme I should apply for?

The admission booklet does not recommend or rank programmes for candidates. To decide which PG Certificate Programme to apply for, you should check the eligibility criteria, programme focus areas, mode of delivery, and other details provided for each programme in the booklet, and apply only for those programmes for which you are eligible. Would you like me to help you check eligibility for a specific programme?
Is there a minimum percentage requirement for eligibility?

Yes, a minimum qualifying percentage is required for eligibility, and it varies depending on the PG Certificate Programme and the candidate’s category. The exact percentage requirements are specified in the admission booklet. Would you like me to look up the percentage for a specific programme?
Can final-year students apply for PG Certificate Programmes?

Yes, final-year students are allowed to apply, provided they complete their qualifying degree and submit the required proof within the timeline specified in the admission booklet. Failure to do so can result in cancellation of admission. Would you like to know the document submission requirements?
Is work experience required to be eligible for PG Certificate Programmes?

No, work experience is not mandatory for most PG Certificate Programmes. Eligibility is primarily based on educational qualifications as specified for each programme in the admission booklet. Would you like to check eligibility conditions for a specific course?
Are diploma holders eligible for PG Certificate Programmes?

Diploma holders without a qualifying degree are generally not eligible for PG Certificate Programmes. Eligibility is based on possession of the required degree qualifications as specified in the admission booklet. Is there anything you want to help me with?
Is the eligibility criteria the same for all PG Certificate Programmes?

No, eligibility criteria vary across PG Certificate Programmes depending on the programme focus and requirements. Each programme’s eligibility conditions are listed separately in the admission booklet. Would you like to review eligibility for a particular programme?
Is there any age limit for applying to PG Certificate Programmes?

The admission booklet does not specify any upper age limit for applying to PG Certificate Programmes. Candidates must meet the educational eligibility criteria. Let me know if you want any other information.
What if I am selected but later found ineligible?

If a candidate is found ineligible at any stage of the admission or document verification process, the admission is liable to be cancelled as per the rules mentioned in the admission booklet. Would you like to know which documents are verified during admission?
Can the eligibility rules change after I apply?

Eligibility rules are governed by the admission booklet applicable to the admission batch. Candidates are required to comply with the eligibility criteria mentioned in that booklet. Any updates or clarifications, if issued, are communicated officially by C-DAC. Is there anything I can help with?
Can non-engineering graduates apply for PG Certificate Programmes?

Yes, non-engineering graduates may be eligible for certain PG Certificate Programmes. As mentioned in the admission booklet, candidates with qualifications such as MCA or MCM, as well as relevant postgraduate degrees in science or mathematics, are eligible for specific programmes. Eligibility depends on the programme applied for. Would you like to check eligibility for a particular PG Certificate Programme?
What is the minimum eligibility criteria common to all PG Certificate Programmes?

As per the admission booklet, the minimum eligibility criteria common to all PG Certificate Programmes require candidates to have either a graduate degree in engineering or technology in relevant disciplines such as IT, Computer Science, Electronics, Telecommunications, Electrical or Instrumentation, or an MSc/MS degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or Electronics. Only candidates who satisfy this minimum eligibility can apply, after which programme-specific eligibility conditions are considered. Would you like to check eligibility for a particular programme?
Can I take admission in more than one PG Certificate Programme at the same time?

No, a candidate can take admission in only one PG Certificate Programme at a time. Although candidates may give preferences for multiple programmes during counselling, only one seat is allotted and can be accepted for admission. Would you like to know how programme preference and seat allotment work during counselling?
How do programme preference and seat allotment work during counselling?

During counselling, candidates are required to submit their programme preferences in order of choice. Seat allotment is carried out based on the candidate’s C-CAT rank in the relevant section, programme preferences, eligibility criteria, and availability of seats. Candidates with higher ranks are considered first according to their preference order. Once a seat is allotted, the candidate must complete the required admission formalities within the specified time to confirm the seat. Is there anything to help with?
I want to know the mode of delivery for a specific programme.
Please specify the PG Certificate program to know the mode of delivery.
Which documents are verified during admission?

During admission, the following documents are verified:

1. Qualifying degree certificate.
2. All mark sheets of the qualifying examination.
3. Proof of having obtained the minimum required percentage of marks.
4. Eligibility-related documents as per the selected course.

All details provided in the application form must match the submitted documents. Is there anything else to help?
I want to look up the percentage for a specific programme.
Please specify the programme to know the percentage.
Which programmes are offered only in physical mode?

The programmes offered only in Fully Physical mode are PGCP-ESD, PGCP-ITISS, PGCP-VLSI, PGCP-MC, PGCP-ASSD, PGCP-HPCSA, and PGCP-RAT. Would you like to know which programmes are offered only in online mode ?
Which programmes are offered only in online mode?
The programmes offered only in Fully Online mode are PGCP-FBD and PGCP-CSF. Would you like to know which programmes are offered in both online and physical modes?
Which programmes are offered in both online and physical modes?

The programmes offered in both Fully Online and Fully Physical modes, depending on the training centre, are PGCP-AC, PGCP-BDA, and PGCP-AI. Would you like to know which programs are offered only in physical mode?
What are the different modes of delivery for PG Certificate Programmes?

PG Certificate Programmes are offered in different modes such as fully online mode, fully physical (on-campus) mode, or both, depending on the specific programme and training centre. Would you like to check the mode of delivery for a particular programme?
Is there any difference between online and physical programmes academically?

According to the admission booklet, the curriculum, assessments, and certification remain the same irrespective of whether a PG Certificate Programme is delivered online or in physical mode. The difference lies only in the mode of delivery. Would you like to know how classes are conducted in online mode?
Do online PG Certificate Programmes require physical presence at any stage?

The admission booklet does not specify mandatory physical presence requirements for online programmes, as operational details may vary by programme or centre. Any such requirements, if applicable, are communicated by C-DAC or the training centre. Would you like to know the general requirements for attending online classes?
What infrastructure do I need to attend online PG Certificate Programmes?

For online PG Certificate Programmes, students are required to have a laptop or desktop computer with a stable internet connection, along with basic audio-visual facilities. Detailed technical requirements, if any, are communicated by the training centre. Would you like to know whether mobile devices are allowed for online classes?
Can I switch from online mode to physical mode after admission?

The admission booklet does not provide any provision for switching between online and physical modes after admission. Mode of delivery is decided at the time of admission based on programme and centre availability. Any exceptions, if permitted, are communicated officially. Would you like to know how the mode is selected during counselling?
Does the mode of delivery affect the course fees?

Yes, the course fees can vary depending on whether the PG Certificate Programme is offered in online or physical mode. The applicable fees for each programme and mode are listed in the admission booklet. Would you like to check the fee details for a specific programme?
I want to check the fee details.
Please specify the programme you want to check the fee details for.
Are hostel or accommodation charges included in the course fee?

No, hostel or accommodation charges are not included in the course fee. Such facilities, if available, are managed by the respective training centres and are charged separately. Details are communicated by the training centre after admission. Would you like to know about hostel availability?
Is the course fee refundable if I withdraw after admission?

Refund of the course fee is governed by the refund and cancellation policy specified in the admission booklet. The amount refundable depends on the stage at which the candidate withdraws from the programme. Would you like to know the detailed refund timeline?
What is the course fee for PGCP-AC?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in Advanced Computing (PGCP-AC) is Rs 76,500 + GST in online mode and Rs 90,000 + GST in physical mode, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the installment details?
What is the course fee for PGCP-BDA?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in Big Data Analytics (PGCP-BDA) is Rs 97,750 + GST in online mode and Rs 1,15,000 + GST in physical mode, as mentioned in the admission booklet. The fee must be paid in two installments. Would you like to know how the installments are structured?
What is the course fee for PGCP-AI?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in Artificial Intelligence (PGCP-AI) is Rs 1,27,500 + GST in online mode and Rs 1,50,000 + GST in physical mode, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the payment schedule?
What is the course fee for PGCP-MC?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in Mobile Computing (PGCP-MC), which is offered in physical mode only, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as mentioned in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the installment amounts?
What is the course fee for PGCP-FBD?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in FinTech & Blockchain Development (PGCP-FBD), which is offered in online mode only, is Rs 1,06,250 + GST, as mentioned in the admission booklet. The fee must be paid in two installments. Would you like to know the installment structure?
What is the course fee for PGCP-CSF?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in Cyber Security & Forensics (PGCP-CSF), which is offered in online mode only, is Rs 76,500 + GST, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the payment schedule?
What is the course fee for PGCP-HPCSA?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in HPC System Administration (PGCP-HPCSA), which is offered in physical mode, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee must be paid in two installments. Would you like to know the payment deadlines?
What is the course fee for PGCP-ASSD?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in Advanced Secure Software Development (PGCP-ASSD), which is offered in physical mode, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as mentioned in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know how the installments are structured?
What is the course fee for PGCP-ITISS?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the PG Certificate Programme in IT Infrastructure, Systems and Security (PGCP-ITISS), which is offered in physical mode, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the installment details?
How is the course fee paid for PG Certificate Programmes?

As per the admission booklet, the course fee for all PG Certificate Programmes is payable in two installments. The first installment is Rs 10,000 + GST and must be paid at the time of seat acceptance. The remaining balance is to be paid as the second installment before the specified deadline. Would you like to know the exact second installment amount for a specific programme?
What if I got a concession on the course fee?

The admission booklet does not mention any provision for fee concessions or discounts for PG Certificate Programmes. Course fees are fixed as specified and must be paid according to the defined installment structure. If any exception or special consideration applies, it is officially communicated by C-DAC. Would you like to know the standard fee and installment details for a specific programme?
What is the course fee for PGCP-RAT?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the Post Graduate Certificate Programme in Robotics & Allied Technologies (PGCP-RAT), which is offered in fully physical mode, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the installment details?
What is the course fee for PGCP-ESD?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the Post Graduate Certificate Programme in Embedded Systems Design (PGCP-ESD), which is offered in fully physical mode, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as mentioned in the admission booklet. The fee must be paid in two installments. Would you like to know the payment schedule?
What is the course fee for PGCP-VLSI?

For the February 2026 batch, the course fee for the Post Graduate Certificate Programme in VLSI Design (PGCP-VLSI), which is offered in fully physical mode, is Rs 90,000 + GST, as specified in the admission booklet. The fee is payable in two installments. Would you like to know the installment details?
Is there a single common rank for all PG Certificate Programmes?

No, there is no single common rank for all PG Certificate Programmes. C-CAT ranks are section-wise, and each programme considers only the rank of its specified section. Would you like to know which C-CAT section applies to which programme?
If I have ranks in multiple C-CAT sections, which one is considered?

Section A of C-CAT is mandatory for all PG Certificate Programmes. For most programmes, qualification in Sections A and B is required, and the corresponding ranks of these sections are considered. For certain programmes in the electronics domain, qualification in all three sections—A, B, and C is required, and ranks of the required sections are used for admission. Only the ranks of the sections specified for a programme are considered, and ranks from other sections are ignored.
Can the C-CAT cut-off change every year?

Yes, C-CAT cut-off criteria may change from year to year based on factors such as the number of candidates, programme demand, and seat availability, as described in the admission booklet. Is there anything to help?
What is the detailed refund timeline for the February 2026 batch?

The detailed refund timeline for the February 2026 batch is as follows:

30 Jan – 4 Feb 2026 (5 pm): ₹1,000 + GST deducted
5 – 10 Feb 2026 (5 pm): ₹5,000 + GST deducted
11 – 24 Feb 2026 (5 pm): ₹10,000 + GST deducted
25 Feb – 24 March 2026: 50% of total course fee + GST deducted
After 24 March 2026: Full fees + GST deducted (No refund)

If a candidate is found non-eligible, ₹10,000 + GST (first installment) will not be refunded.
If proof of qualification is not submitted by 30 June 2026, no refund will be given. Is there anything else to help?
What is the process for selecting the mode (online or physical) during counselling?

The mode (Online or Physical) is determined based on the course selected, the training centre offering the course, seat availability, C-CAT rank, and the order of preferences filled during counselling. Seat allocation is done strictly based on C-CAT rank and course-centre preferences. If a course is offered in Fully Online mode at a selected centre, it will be conducted online. If offered in Fully Physical mode at a selected centre, it will be conducted physically. Mode cannot be changed manually after seat allocation. Let me know if you want any help.
What is the breakdown of duration of PG-Certificate programme?

The structure of classes is as follows:

Duration: 24 weeks
Total Hours: 1200 hours (900 hours theory + lab + project, 300 hours self study)
Classes: 6–8 hours per day
Days: 6 days per week

Centralised Mid-Course Examination (CMCE): May 2026
Centralised Course-End Examination (CCEE): July 2026
Examinations are conducted physically at designated centres.

Minimum 85% attendance is mandatory to be eligible for CMCE and CCEE.

Students must have a laptop/PC, high-speed uninterrupted internet connection, webcam, and microphone. Is there anything you want to know?
What is the hostel availability policy for physical courses?

Hostel facilities are available for students enrolled in physical courses at selected training centres. Hostel allotment is on a first-come, first-served basis. Outstation students are given preference. Requests must be sent after admission lists are published. Some centres without hostel facilities may assist students in finding suitable accommodation. Would you like to know which centers provide hostel facilities?
Which centres provide hostel facilities?

The following training centres provide hostel facilities for physical courses:

AIT (YCC), Mumbai
C-DAC, Bengaluru
C-DAC, Hyderabad (only for girls)
C-DAC, Mumbai (Kharghar)
C-DAC, New Delhi
C-DAC, Noida
C-DAC, Pune
IACSD, Pune
MET-IIT, Mumbai
MET-IIT, Nashik
Sunbeam, Karad
Sunbeam, Pune
USM’s Shriram Mantri VITA, Mumbai

Hostel allotment is on a first-come, first-served basis, with preference given to outstation students. Is there anything to help with?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Advanced Computing (PGCP-AC)?

PGCP-AC is delivered in Fully Online or Fully Physical mode depending on the training centre offering the course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-AC in online or physical mode?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Big Data Analytics (PGCP-BDA)?

PGCP-BDA is delivered in Fully Online or Fully Physical mode depending on the training centre offering the course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-BDA in online or physical mode?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Artificial Intelligence (PGCP-AI)?

PGCP-AI is delivered in Fully Online or Fully Physical mode depending on the training centre offering the course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-AI in online or physical mode?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Embedded Systems Design (PGCP-ESD)?
PGCP-ESD is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-ESD?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in IT Infrastructure, Systems & Security (PGCP-ITISS)?
PGCP-ITISS is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-ITISS?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in VLSI Design (PGCP-VLSI)?
PGCP-VLSI is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-VLSI?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Mobile Computing (PGCP-MC)?
PGCP-MC is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-MC?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Advanced Secure Software Development (PGCP-ASSD)?
PGCP-ASSD is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-ASSD?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in HPC System Administration (PGCP-HPCSA)?
PGCP-HPCSA is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-HPCSA?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Robotics & Allied Technologies (PGCP-RAT)?
PGCP-RAT is delivered in Fully Physical mode at all training centres offering this course. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-RAT?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in FinTech & Blockchain Development (PGCP-FBD)?
PGCP-FBD is delivered in Fully Online mode only. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-FBD?
What is the mode of delivery of PG Certificate Programme in Cyber Security & Forensics (PGCP-CSF)?
PGCP-CSF is delivered in Fully Online mode only. Would you like to know which specific centres offer PGCP-CSF?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-AC?

At Bengaluru, PGCP-AC is offered in Fully Physical mode. At Chennai it is offered in Fully Online mode. At Kolkata, Mumbai (Kharghar), New Delhi, Noida, Patna, and Thiruvananthapuram it is offered in both Fully Online and Fully Physical modes. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-AC at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-BDA?

At Bengaluru and Noida, PGCP-BDA is offered in Fully Physical mode. At Chennai it is offered in Fully Online mode. At Mumbai (Kharghar), New Delhi, Patna, and Thiruvananthapuram it is offered in both Fully Online and Fully Physical modes. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-BDA at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-AI?

At Bengaluru and Noida, PGCP-AI is offered in Fully Physical mode. At Chennai it is offered in Fully Online mode. At Mumbai (Kharghar), New Delhi, Patna, and Thiruvananthapuram it is offered in both Fully Online and Fully Physical modes. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-AI at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-ESD?
PGCP-ESD is offered in Fully Physical mode at Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Noida, and Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-ESD at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-ITISS?
PGCP-ITISS is offered in Fully Physical mode at Hyderabad, Noida, and Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-ITISS at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-VLSI?
PGCP-VLSI is offered in Fully Physical mode at Hyderabad and Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-VLSI at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-MC?
PGCP-MC is offered in Fully Physical mode at Hyderabad and Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-MC at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-ASSD?
PGCP-ASSD is offered in Fully Physical mode at Noida and Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-ASSD at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-HPCSA?
PGCP-HPCSA is offered in Fully Physical mode at Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-HPCSA at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-RAT?
PGCP-RAT is offered in Fully Physical mode at Bengaluru and Pune. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-RAT at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-FBD?
PGCP-FBD is offered in Fully Online mode at Chennai, Mumbai (Kharghar), New Delhi, Patna, and Thiruvananthapuram. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-FBD at any specific centre?
Which specific centres offer PGCP-CSF?

PGCP-CSF is offered in Fully Online mode at Chennai, Mumbai (Kharghar), New Delhi, Patna, Guwahati, and Thiruvananthapuram. Would you like to know the seat capacity for PGCP-CSF at any specific centre?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-AC at different centres?

For PGCP-AC, the seat capacity is as follows: Bengaluru – 240 (Physical), Chennai – 80 (Online), Kolkata – 60 (Online) and 60 (Physical), Mumbai (Kharghar) – 260 (Online) and 90 (Physical), New Delhi – 40 (Online) and 40 (Physical), Noida – 160 (Physical), Patna – 60 (Online), Thiruvananthapuram – 90 (Online). Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-AC?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-BDA at different centres?

For PGCP-BDA, the seat capacity is as follows: Bengaluru – 120 (Physical), Chennai – 60 (Online), Mumbai (Kharghar) – 60 (Physical), New Delhi – 40 (Online), Noida – 60 (Physical), Patna – 60 (Online), Thiruvananthapuram – 60 (Online). Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-BDA?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-AI at different centres?

For PGCP-AI, the seat capacity is as follows: Bengaluru – 120 (Physical), Chennai – 60 (Online), Hyderabad – 120 (Physical), Noida – 60 (Physical), Patna – 60 (Online), Thiruvananthapuram – 60 (Online). Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-AI?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-ESD at different centres?
For PGCP-ESD, the seat capacity is as follows: Bengaluru – 60, Hyderabad – 40, Noida – 40, Pune – 60. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-ESD?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-ITISS at different centres?
For PGCP-ITISS, the seat capacity is as follows: Hyderabad – 40, Noida – 40, Pune – 60. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-ITISS?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-VLSI at different centres?
For PGCP-VLSI, the seat capacity is as follows: Hyderabad – 80, Pune – 40. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-VLSI?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-MC at different centres?
For PGCP-MC, the seat capacity is as follows: Hyderabad – 40, Pune – 30. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-MC?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-ASSD at different centres?
For PGCP-ASSD, the seat capacity is as follows: Noida – 40, Pune – 60. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-ASSD?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-HPCSA at different centres?
For PGCP-HPCSA, the seat capacity is as follows: Pune – 60. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-HPCSA?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-RAT at different centres?
For PGCP-RAT, the seat capacity is as follows: Bengaluru – 60, Pune – 120. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-RAT?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-FBD at different centres?
For PGCP-FBD, the seat capacity is as follows: Mumbai (Kharghar) – 260, New Delhi – 120, Patna – 60. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-FBD?
What is the seat capacity for PGCP-CSF at different centres?

For PGCP-CSF, the seat capacity is as follows: Chennai – 60, Mumbai (Kharghar) – 260, New Delhi – 40, Patna – 60, Guwahati – 40, Thiruvananthapuram – 60. Would you like to know the total course fee for PGCP-CSF?
What is the eligibility criteria for PGCP-AC?

Minimum 55% marks. Graduate in Engineering or Technology (IT/Computer Science/Electronics/Telecommunications/Electrical/Instrumentation), OR MSc/MS in Computer Science/IT/Electronics, OR MCA/MCM, OR Post Graduate Degree in Physics/Mathematics/Statistics, OR Post Graduate Degree in Management with graduation in IT/Computer Science/Computer Applications. Would you like to know which C-CAT sections are required for this programme?
What is the eligibility criteria for PGCP-BDA?

Minimum 55% marks. Graduate in Engineering or Technology, OR MSc/MS in Computer Science/IT/Electronics, OR MCA/MCM, OR Post Graduate Degree in Physics/Mathematics/Statistics, OR Post Graduate Degree in Management with graduation in IT/Computer Science/Computer Applications. Would you like to know which C-CAT sections are required for this programme?
What is the eligibility criteria for PGCP-AI?

What is the complete process to apply for C-CAT?

The complete process to apply for C-CAT is as follows:

Step 1: Register online on www.cdac.in or acts.cdac.in and obtain your Form Number.
Step 2: Log in using the Form Number and fill personal and qualifying degree details.
Step 3: Select the course category (II or III).
Step 4: Upload a clear passport-size photograph.
Step 5: Select three preferred examination cities.
Step 6: Pay the C-CAT examination fee online.

After successful payment, candidates should download and save the filled application form for records. Would you like to know the C-CAT application fee details?
What is the C-CAT application fee?

The C-CAT fee (inclusive of GST) is as follows:

Category II (Sections A+B): Rs 1550/-
Category III (Sections A+B+C): Rs 1750/-

The fee must be paid online using net banking, UPI, or credit/debit cards. The C-CAT fee once paid is not refundable. Would you like to know the payment modes in detail?
How can the C-CAT examination fee be paid?

The C-CAT examination fee must be paid online through the payment gateway available in the application portal. Payment can be made using:

Net banking
UPI
Credit card
Debit card

After successful payment, the transaction details will be updated in the application status. Would you like to know what happens if the payment is not completed before the deadline?
Can I change the course category after submitting the C-CAT application form?

No, once the C-CAT application form is submitted online, candidates cannot change the selected course category (II or III). Therefore, candidates must carefully select the appropriate category before final submission. Would you like to know what happens if incorrect information is submitted in the application form?
What happens if I submit incorrect information in the C-CAT application?

All details entered in the registration and application forms must match the original documents submitted at the time of admission. If a candidate is found non-eligible or has provided incorrect information at any stage of the admission process or course delivery, the admission will be cancelled with immediate effect. Would you like to know how to download the C-CAT admit card?
When and how can I download the C-CAT admit card?

C-CAT admit cards can be downloaded from the C-DAC website during the announced dates by logging in using the Form Number and password. The admit card contains the venue, date, and time of the examination. Candidates must carry a printed admit card along with a valid photo identity proof to the test centre. No candidate will be allowed to appear without a valid admit card. Would you like to know the important dates related to C-CAT?
What are the important dates for C-CAT and admission for the February 2026 batch?

Important dates for the February 2026 batch are:

Beginning of Online Registration: 27 November 2025
Closing of Registration and Fee Payment: 29 December 2025
Admit Card Download: 6–10 January 2026
C-CAT Exam Dates: 10 and 11 January 2026
Result Announcement: 22 January 2026
Course Commencement: 25 February 2026

Candidates must strictly follow these deadlines as no extensions are guaranteed. Would you like to know the C-CAT exam pattern and marking scheme?
What is the exam pattern of C-CAT?

C-CAT consists of three sections: Section A, Section B, and Section C.

Each section contains 50 multiple-choice questions.
Each question carries 3 marks.
For every incorrect answer, 1 mark is deducted.
Unanswered questions carry 0 marks.

Category II candidates attempt Sections A and B.
Category III candidates attempt Sections A, B, and C.

Each section is conducted for 1 hour.
Total duration is 2 hours for Category II and 3 hours for Category III.

Would you like to know the syllabus coverage for these sections?
What is the syllabus coverage for C-CAT sections?

Section A covers English, Quantitative Aptitude, Logical Reasoning, and Computer Fundamentals.

Section B covers C Programming, Data Structures, Operating Systems, and Computer Networks.

Section C covers Advanced topics including Algorithms, Advanced Data Structures, Computer Architecture, and Digital Electronics.

Would you like to know the total maximum marks for each category?
What is the total maximum marks for Category II and Category III in C-CAT?

Each section has 50 questions worth 3 marks each, so the maximum marks per section is 150.

Category II (Sections A and B) has a maximum of 300 marks.
Category III (Sections A, B, and C) has a maximum of 450 marks.

Would you like to know how negative marking affects the total score?
How does negative marking affect the C-CAT score?

For every incorrect answer in C-CAT, 1 mark is deducted. Correct answers receive 3 marks, and unanswered questions receive 0 marks.

The final score is calculated as:
(Total correct answers × 3) − (Total incorrect answers × 1).

Would you like to know the duration of the C-CAT examination?
What is the duration of the C-CAT examination?

Each section of C-CAT is conducted for 1 hour.

Category II candidates (Sections A and B) have a total duration of 2 hours.
Category III candidates (Sections A, B, and C) have a total duration of 3 hours.

Would you like to know how sections are mapped to different programmes?
Which programmes fall under Category II in C-CAT?

The programmes under Category II (Sections A and B) are:

PGCP-AC
PGCP-BDA
PGCP-AI
PGCP-MC
PGCP-ASSD
PGCP-FBD
PGCP-CSF

Candidates applying for these programmes must attempt Section A and Section B of C-CAT.

Would you like to know which programmes fall under Category III?
Which programmes fall under Category III in C-CAT?

The programmes under Category III (Sections A, B, and C) are:

PGCP-ESD
PGCP-ITISS
PGCP-VLSI
PGCP-HPCSA
PGCP-RAT

Candidates applying for these programmes must attempt Sections A, B, and C of C-CAT.

Would you like to know how to select the correct C-CAT category while applying?
How should I select the correct C-CAT category while applying?

Candidates must select the C-CAT category based on the programme they intend to apply for.

If applying for a Category II programme, select Category II and attempt Sections A and B.
If applying for a Category III programme, select Category III and attempt Sections A, B, and C.

The selected category cannot be changed after submission of the application form.

Would you like to know what happens if a candidate selects the wrong category?
What happens if I select the wrong C-CAT category while applying?

If a candidate selects the wrong C-CAT category, they will be required to attempt the sections corresponding to that category. This may lead to an inability to apply for the intended programme if the sections attempted do not match the programme requirements. Since category selection cannot be changed after submission, it is crucial to select the correct category at the time of application. Is there anything to help with?
What happens if two candidates obtain the same marks in C-CAT?

If two or more candidates obtain the same total marks in C-CAT, tie-breaking rules are applied to determine the merit ranking.

The ranking is decided based on predefined section-wise priority rules.

Would you like to know the exact tie-breaking criteria used in C-CAT?
What are the tie-breaking criteria used in C-CAT?

If candidates have equal total marks in C-CAT, the following criteria are applied sequentially:

1. Higher marks in Section B are given preference.
2. If still tied, higher marks in Section A are considered.
3. If still tied, higher marks in Section C are considered.

If the tie persists after applying section-wise comparison, candidates may share the same rank.

Would you like to know how C-CAT ranks are generated from section scores?
How are C-CAT ranks generated from section scores?

C-CAT ranks are generated based on the total marks obtained in the required sections for the selected category.

For Category II, total marks of Sections A and B are considered.
For Category III, total marks of Sections A, B, and C are considered.

Candidates are ranked in descending order of total marks after applying tie-breaking rules where necessary.

Would you like to know whether C-CAT rank is valid for future batches?
Is C-CAT rank valid for future batches?

No, the C-CAT rank is valid only for the specific admission batch for which the examination was conducted. It cannot be carried forward to future batches.

Would you like to know whether a candidate can appear for C-CAT multiple times?
Can a candidate appear for C-CAT multiple times?

Yes, a candidate can appear for C-CAT in multiple admission cycles. Each attempt is treated independently, and a new rank is generated for each examination.

Would you like to know whether the best score from multiple attempts is considered?
Is the best score considered if a candidate appears multiple times for C-CAT?

No, C-CAT does not combine or compare scores from different attempts. Each examination cycle generates an independent rank that is valid only for that specific batch.

Would you like to know whether there is any restriction on the number of attempts?
Is there any restriction on the number of attempts for C-CAT?

There is no specific restriction on the number of times a candidate can appear for C-CAT. However, each attempt requires a fresh registration and payment of the applicable examination fee.

Would you like to know the eligibility verification process during admission?
At what stage is eligibility verified during the admission process?

Eligibility is verified during the admission confirmation stage after seat allocation and before final enrollment. Candidates must produce original documents for scrutiny at the allotted training centre. Admission remains provisional until verification is successfully completed.

Would you like to know what happens if a candidate fails to produce required documents at the time of verification?
What happens if a candidate fails to produce required documents during verification?

If a candidate fails to produce the required original documents during verification, the admission will not be confirmed. The seat may be cancelled, and the candidate may forfeit the allotted seat as per admission rules.

Would you like to know whether provisional admission is allowed in case of pending results?
Is provisional admission allowed if qualifying examination results are awaited?

Provisional admission may be permitted for candidates whose qualifying examination results are awaited, provided they submit proof of having appeared for the examination. However, final admission confirmation is subject to submission of the qualifying degree certificate and proof of required marks within the stipulated timeline.

Would you like to know what happens if the required percentage is not obtained after result declaration?
What happens if a candidate does not obtain the required percentage after result declaration?

If a candidate fails to obtain the required percentage of marks in the qualifying examination, the admission will be cancelled even if provisional admission was granted earlier. Eligibility conditions must be satisfied at the time of final verification.

Would you like to know whether providing false information affects admission at later stages?
Can admission be cancelled if false information is discovered after enrollment?

Yes, if at any stage it is found that a candidate has provided false information, suppressed facts, or submitted invalid documents, the admission may be cancelled immediately. Such action can be taken even after the course has commenced, as eligibility compliance is mandatory throughout the admission process.

Would you like to know about attendance requirements during the programme?
What certificate is awarded after successful completion of the PG programme?

Upon successful completion of the programme and fulfillment of all academic and attendance requirements, candidates are awarded a Post Graduate Certificate issued by C-DAC. The certificate is granted only after satisfactory performance in all prescribed modules and evaluations.

Would you like to know whether this certificate is equivalent to a university degree?
Is the C-DAC PG certificate equivalent to a university degree?

The Post Graduate Certificate awarded by C-DAC is a professional certification programme and is not equivalent to a university degree such as M.Tech or MSc. It is a skill-oriented industry-focused certification.

Would you like to know under what conditions the certificate may not be awarded?
Under what conditions may the certificate not be awarded?

The certificate may not be awarded if a candidate fails to meet minimum attendance requirements, does not successfully complete required evaluations, violates academic regulations, or fails to clear mandatory assessments. All programme conditions must be satisfied for certification.

Would you like to know about placement support provided after course completion?
Does C-DAC provide placement support after completion of the programme?

C-DAC provides placement assistance to eligible candidates through its centralized placement activities. Placement support is offered to candidates who successfully complete the programme and meet academic and attendance requirements.

However, placement is not guaranteed and depends on candidate performance and recruiter requirements.

Would you like to know whether online mode candidates are eligible for placement activities?
Are candidates enrolled in online mode eligible for placement activities?

Yes, candidates enrolled in fully online mode are eligible to participate in centralized placement activities, subject to fulfilling programme completion and eligibility requirements.

Would you like to know how placement eligibility is determined?
How is placement eligibility determined?

Placement eligibility is determined based on successful completion of all academic requirements, maintaining the required attendance, and meeting any additional criteria specified by recruiters. Participation in placement activities is subject to compliance with institutional guidelines.

Would you like to know about discipline and conduct rules during the programme?
Can admission be cancelled for disciplinary reasons during the programme?

Yes, admission may be cancelled if a candidate is found guilty of misconduct, indiscipline, violation of institutional rules, or any act that disrupts academic or administrative functioning. C-DAC reserves the right to take appropriate disciplinary action, including cancellation of admission.

Would you like to know whether fees are refundable in such cases?
Are fees refundable if admission is cancelled due to misconduct?

If admission is cancelled due to misconduct, submission of false information, or violation of rules, refund of fees is governed strictly by the official refund policy. In such cases, candidates may forfeit part or full fees as per the applicable refund schedule.

Would you like to know whether C-DAC reserves the right to modify admission rules?
Does C-DAC reserve the right to modify admission rules?

Yes, C-DAC reserves the right to modify, amend, or update admission policies, rules, schedules, and programme details at its discretion. Candidates are advised to regularly check official notifications for updates.

Would you like to know about any change-of-centre or programme transfer policies?
Is change of training centre allowed after admission?

Change of training centre after admission is not generally permitted. Centre allocation is based on merit, availability of seats, and programme selection. Once admission is confirmed, candidates are expected to continue at the allotted centre.

Any exceptional request for change is subject to institutional approval and seat availability.

Would you like to know whether programme change or upgrade is allowed after admission?
Is programme change or upgrade allowed after admission?

Programme change or upgrade after admission is not normally permitted. Admission is granted based on merit ranking, seat availability, and programme preference submitted during the admission process.

Any request for change is subject to institutional rules and may not be entertained once the programme has commenced.

Would you like to know whether switching between online and physical mode is allowed?
Is switching between online and physical mode allowed after admission?

Switching between fully online and fully physical mode after admission is generally not permitted. Mode selection is finalized during the admission process and is linked to centre allocation and seat availability.

Any modification is subject to institutional discretion and availability of seats in the requested mode.

Would you like to know about general legal and compliance conditions applicable to admission?
Can I edit my C-CAT application form after submission?

Once the C-CAT application form is finally submitted, major details such as selected category and course preferences cannot be changed. Candidates must review all information carefully before submission.

Would you like to know what happens if incorrect information is entered?
Can the selected examination city be changed after submitting the application?

Examination city preferences are selected during the application process. Change of examination city after submission is generally not permitted and is subject to administrative feasibility.

Would you like to know how examination centres are allotted?
What happens if the first installment is not paid within the deadline?

If the first installment is not paid within the stipulated deadline, the allotted seat may be cancelled automatically. Seat confirmation is subject to timely payment of the prescribed installment amount.

Would you like to know whether the cancelled seat can be restored later?
How can candidates contact C-DAC for admission-related queries?

Candidates can contact C-DAC through the official website and designated admission helpdesk channels mentioned in the admission notification. All official communication is provided through authorized portals and contact points.

Would you like to know where official notifications are published?
How are examination centres allotted?

Examination centres are allotted based on the candidate’s selected city preferences and availability of seats at the respective test centres. The final allotted centre is mentioned in the admit card. Is there anything you want to help with?
Can a cancelled seat be restored after missing the installment deadline?

Once a seat is cancelled due to non-payment within the stipulated deadline, restoration is not guaranteed. Any reconsideration is subject to seat availability and institutional discretion. Is there anything else you would like to know?
Where are official admission notifications published?

All official admission notifications, schedules, and updates are published on the official C-DAC website and the ACTS admission portal. Candidates are advised to refer only to authorized sources for accurate information.Let me know if you need any further clarification.
How can I contact ACTS Pune for admission queries?

Candidates may visit the official C-DAC website at https://www.cdac.in or the ACTS admission portal at https://acts.cdac.in for official notifications and updates. For admission-related queries, candidates may contact the ACTS admission helpdesk through the contact details provided on the official website.
